transcript:
HCP: right, so I'm going to refer you to Rheumatology today
HCP: your name is Sarah Jenkins
HCP: you're 34 years old
HCP: the main issue is persistent joint swelling in your hands
HCP: this has been going on for about three months now
HCP: it's particularly bad in the mornings when you wake up
HCP: you also have a history of psoriasis on your elbows
HCP: your mother has rheumatoid arthritis
HCP: currently you are taking ibuprofen 400mg three times a day
HCP: you've tried paracetamol but it doesn't help the swelling much
HCP: you also mentioned feeling quite tired lately
HCP: no known drug allergies
HCP: I want to get this sorted out properly
HCP: please send them the relevant bloods and X-rays
HCP: thanks

transcript:
HCP: Hi Sarah, thanks for coming in today.
HCP: I see you're here for your recurring knee pain and that persistent cough.
HCP: regarding the knee, it's been aching for about three weeks now, especially when climbing stairs.
HCP: you've had physio before but the pain keeps coming back.
HCP: as for the cough, it started last month and hasn't settled despite taking panadol.
HCP: I'm also noting you're on ventolin for your asthma.
HCP: let's refer you to Orthopaedics for the knee and Respiratory for the cough.
HCP: please send these letters through.
Patient: Thanks Doctor, I'll get the forms signed.
HCP: You should feel better soon once the specialists see you.

reason.text: recurring knee pain and persistent cough

transcript:
HCP: right, so let's get this referral sorted out
HCP: patient is David Chen, currently 34 years old
HCP: he comes in complaining of persistent ankle swelling
HCP: he says it's been going on for about three months now
HCP: the swelling gets worse later in the day and when he climbs stairs
HCP: he's tried some elevation at home but it hasn't helped much
HCP: there's no history of trauma or recent injury to the leg
HCP: he does have a family history of varicose veins in his mother
HCP: currently taking panadol for occasional knee discomfort
HCP: no known drug allergies and no other chronic conditions
HCP: physical exam shows mild pitting edema bilaterally
HCP: please refer to Orthopaedics for assessment and management

transcript:
HCP: Hi Sarah, glad to see you.
HCP: I'm sending you to a Rheumatologist today.
HCP: You've been dealing with persistent joint swelling in your left knee for about three weeks.
HCP: You mentioned it started after a mild fall in the garden.
HCP: Your history includes a previous episode of gout two years ago.
HCP: You are currently taking ibuprofen 400mg with food as needed.
HCP: No other regular medications or allergies.
HCP: Please ask the specialist to investigate this swelling and adjust your pain plan.
